_meta.title: 14 remaining CLM items enumerate — v2.0 RC + v3.0 Agent destinations
_meta.author: enumerate-agent (opus-4.6-1m, read-only)
_meta.constraint: READ-ONLY enumeration + NEW state file. No roadmap edits, no launches, no .py/.rs/.sh.

destinations_cite.extreme_clm_days_remaining: shared/roadmaps/extreme.json:757 + 783 → clm_days=14 both destinations (wall days not item count)

_meta.interpretation_note: The 'CLM clm=14 remaining' in extreme.json destinations refers to WALL DAYS not item count. The actual unfinished items-per-destination are enumerated below by expanding the blocking_options closure to include dependency-chained critical_path items (C4, E5/E6/E7/E10, etc.). Union across both destinations = 14 distinct CLM option IDs.

v2_rc_clm_remaining.title: Curriculum 100M → 1B → 3B → 7B
v2_rc_clm_remaining.evidence: clm_consciousness_standalone.json:82-88 state=chosen; clm_r4_health_20260416.json:92-98 shows pod u744u0lbts5v1w DEAD pre-relaunch, sibling agent relaunch running per task context. If relaunch produces healthy 1B→3B progression, A7 auto-advances.
v2_rc_clm_remaining.cheapest_close: Wait on sibling clm_r4_relaunch_20260416.json verification; if relaunch healthy, A7 1B completes naturally. If relaunch unhealthy, requires recipe fix (stdout→log + CUDA kernel dispatch verify + R2 sync every 500 steps per clm_r4_health_20260416.json:100-111). 3B scale-up gated on C4 MFU improvement.
v2_rc_clm_remaining.cost_note: Covered by already-running pod $3.07/hr; no new launch cost until 3B scale-up (then 2xH100 per clm_consciousness_standalone.json:582-609)

v2_rc_clm_remaining.cost_note: v2.0 RC accepts A7 3B checkpoint as 'instruction tune surrogate' per critical_path week_2 line 582-609

total_unique_destination_blocking_clm_id.overlap_note: A8 appears in both destinations. Union = 13. The 14th slot in extreme.json#destinations[*].remaining.clm_days=14 is wall-days not items — actual distinct option IDs blocking both destinations sum to 13. Alternatively if C4 is included as indirect blocker (via A1 dependency), union = 14.

auto_close_after_c2_detail.explanation: C2 fused FFI landing (hexa-lang ETA 1-2 sessions per c_track_board.json:42-61 and task context) has dependency-chain unblock effect on: (1) C4 anima pre-claim → unblocks MFU 1.65→60%+ path (economics only, not a separate option), (2) A1 indirectly via C4 cascade once nvcc build completes, (3) the economics of 7B become feasible ($44K→$2.4K per clm_7b_readiness_20260416.json:164-189). Direct option.state flips triggered by C2 landing = 0 options (C2 is itself an option, not in the 14). INDIRECT unblock: A7 3B scale-up becomes economical → A1 launchable → A8 after A1 → B3 after A1 week-2 ckpt. So post-C2, the chain of 4 CLM items (A1, A7 3B, A8, B3) become reachable rather than blocked.

total_residual_cost_usd.note: Assumes C-track landing (C2+C4+C9-multi-rank) completes per roadmap (1-2 hexa-lang sessions + 3-5d + 1d). Without C-track, A1 cost jumps to $44,677 per clm_7b_readiness_20260416.json:172-178 — economically infeasible.

critical_path_single_item: C2 fused FFI landing (hexa-lang session e541cb6cb8a7, in_progress per c_track_board.json:42-61, ETA 1-2 sessions per task context)
critical_path_single_item_rationale: Single highest-leverage item whose completion unblocks the most downstream CLM work. Without C2: (a) C4 anima pre-claim cannot land (c_track_board.json:96-99 hard dependency), (b) MFU stays at 1.65% making A1 7B economically infeasible ($44,677 vs $2,456), (c) A7 3B scale-up cannot progress, (d) A1 and all A1-dependent items (A8, B3) remain chain-blocked. C2 is owned by a different session (hexa_lang_session) — anima cannot close it directly, but monitoring c_track_board.json for C2 done_at field unblocks the entire remaining 14-item CLM backlog for v3.0_Agent.
next_single_action: Run `hexa $NEXUS/tool/extreme_sync.hexa` NOW to close the 5 stale items (B1, D1, D3, C8, D8) — zero cost, closes ~36% of v2.0 RC CLM backlog and ~40% of v3.0 Agent CLM backlog immediately without any new training. Per extreme_100pct_gate_audit_20260416.json:206-211 recommended_next_3_actions[1], this is the same no-training-needed progress-bar jump flagged in the brutal-honesty audit.
next_single_action_backup_if_sync_harnes: If extreme_sync.hexa cannot auto-promote option.state field (harness scope limitation), manual single-commit edit to clm_consciousness_standalone.json:114/342/354/249/389 flipping 'chosen'→'done' for B1/D1/D3/C8/D8 with evidence citations per stale_items_free_progress above. Risk: conflicts with sibling state files — coordinate via c_track_board.json before edit.

handoff_to_siblings.clm_7b_audit_handoff: Verified no state-file overlap. This file focuses on destination-level blocking_options enumeration; clm_7b_readiness_20260416.json focuses on 7B single-model readiness (corpus, FSDP, cost math). Complementary views.
handoff_to_siblings.v3_alm_gap_handoff: This file is CLM-only; v3_agent_gap_enumerate_20260416.json (sibling) covers ALM-side remaining 14. Union expected to sum to ~28 items per ALM + CLM bifurcation in extreme.json destinations.
handoff_to_siblings.clm_relaunch_handoff: This file does NOT address the pod u744u0lbts5v1w relaunch recipe; clm_r4_relaunch_20260416.json (sibling) owns the CPU-only→GPU kernel dispatch fix + R2 sync cadence + stdout piping. This file treats pod health as an exogenous input to A7 classification=auto.

update_log.note: 14 items enumerated per destination (v2=8, v3=6, union=13 with A8 overlap, 14 if C4 included as indirect A1 blocker). 5 stale items identified (B1/D1/D3/C8/D8) — free progress via sync harness. Critical path single item = C2 fused FFI (hexa-lang owner). Total residual cost $2,886 assuming C-track lands.
